更新於: 2026-07-13 09:17 ▶ 原片
@derbyct864 ❤️ 5.6k
Its hard to listen to parents being told their child is dead..no matter how old their child is, they will always be their child.
@DangerEditzREAL ❤️ 4.8k
Man, the fact that her parents were waiting for her to come over for dinner makes it even sadder.
@ZoeyHalle-i6b ❤️ 4.1k
I was devastated to hear her father say they were waiting for her for supper. What a terrible way to end things. rip, Kristen.
@IratePuffin ❤️ 1.9k
My kids and I were stalked by a mountain lion like 20 years ago. That moment when you realize you’re prey is hard to describe to someone who’s never felt it. Gives me chills just thinking about it!
@K90W ❤️ 1.8k
Running a football field in 5 seconds is fucking nuts!
@aikumaDK ❤️ 1.6k
I've seen videos of people zooming in on a mountain lion that's stalking them. They're practically wearing a ghillie suit with how difficult they were to be seen.
@ChefSuperior ❤️ 1.6k
always carry a weapon if you're going on a hike.
@Aurora-do2rv ❤️ 1.5k
“We’re waiting for our daughter to come over for dinner”. The way her father said that, is heartbreaking. He’s still hoping she’s just a little late for dinner. rip 💔💜
@glanza-87 ❤️ 1.2k
That cop not telling them it was a animal attack when they asked if she'd been murdered, praise to the chaplain for giving them the information they were desperate for.
@scottulrich2725 ❤️ 1.1k
I live in the mountains of Montana. I've been bluff charged by grizzlies twice. The number of people who wander around unarmed, without a clue in large predator country is astounding. I had to participate in two grizzly attack recoveries -- in one of them, their bear spray canister had bite marks.

Large caliber is the only equalizer.
@cassiusvoidkin ❤️ 1.1k
I heard a mountain lion cry when I was alone in the dark in the country. One of the most genuinely horrifying things you will ever hear. I immediately knew exactly why our ancestors believed in devils and evil spirits.
@as_below_so_above ❤️ 997
It is absolutely terrifying when you know there is a mountain lion, but I can't imagine the horror of being taken surprise by one.
@selmamarly ❤️ 907
That was the largest mountain lion they'd ever seen, and it hadn't even reached adulthood. I don't think Kristen had a chance against such a huge monster
@LadyHardcore ❤️ 665
I am so terribly sorry for her and her parents. Hearing them learn the news is just crushing. May she rest in peace.
@vintage1950 ❤️ 639
This was on our news here in the uk. So sad, rest well Kristin.
@featherlilli ❤️ 637
Notice how it's dead quiet where there would be other animals and birds singing outside. That's how you know predators are hunting. What a heartbreaking case..
@Spatula52 ❤️ 541
It’s a lose-lose situation. I’m sad for the humans and sad for the mountain lions.
@CS-bu9kd ❤️ 505
You wouldn't catch me in those woods ever, especially alone .
@peterwilson5528 ❤️ 473
Out in the wilderness, you are at the mercy of nature. This was a terribly rare event. R.I.P. Kristen Kovatch.
@Chaostick ❤️ 366
Rabies isn't the main reason a mountain lion would attack. They will hunt people as a last resort if their food source is limited. Since there were 3 cats within the same area, they were probably competing for food and one got desperate enough to attack humans.
@calientemac1 ❤️ 293
A few years ago, my son and I were alone hiking on a trail on Mt Rainier when we kept feeling like something was watching us. After hearing the sound of crackling in the foliage, we decided to come down from the trail. Out of all the trails we hiked on that day, that’s the only trail I felt something was lurking and watching us. I’m still glad we went with our gut feeling.
@metroscene ❤️ 286
“To my right or left?” “To-your nine o’clock!”
Pinky and the brain here yall
@Name-s7w8v ❤️ 267
If these hikers did not find her so quickly after her death, she would have become another missing person.
@nokturnalhunter ❤️ 265
I really admire how these cops are capable of informing parents about these things regularly, the moment the father spoke about them waiting for her to dine i had instantly tears in my eyes and i am a grown ass man. Rest in peace.
@poonongawtti ❤️ 250
too many people dont realize all the different things cops have to do, breaking news like that can really bring you down, no matter who you are. God bless all the good cops.
@ludwiglanestudios ❤️ 244
The 2nd mountain lion that was shot that had nothing to do with the attack was like, “tf did I do?”
@Alex_frost1994 ❤️ 235
Losing a loved one in New Year. Sometimes life can be so unfair. rip young woman.
@Powerwagon79440 ❤️ 216
Running alone in the woods probably triggered the big cats hunting instincts.
@jamesfox248 ❤️ 212
Telling people that mountain lions don't normally attack people gives a sense of security, but all apex predators will kill and eat you if given the chance. Its sad what happened to her.
@InteriorDesignStudent ❤️ 200
That was the largest mountain lion they'd ever seen, and it wasn't even an adult yet. I don't think Kristen had a chance against that large of a predator.
